tex: Write down the temperature that is 7 °C below -3 °C.
Calculate $256 \times 0.25 + 4 \div 8$.

Here is a list of numbers.
87

77

57

47

27

From this list, write down

(a) a cube number,

(b) a prime number.
Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 84 and 105.

Write in standard form.

(a) $72000$

(b) $0.0018$
Expand and simplify $(x + 3)(x + 5)$.
Find the gradient of the line that is perpendicular to the line $2y = 3 + 5x$.

When $\sin x^\circ = 0.36$, find

(a) the acute angle $x^\circ$,

(b) the obtuse angle $x^\circ$.

A cuboid measures 5 cm by 7 cm by 9.5 cm.

Work out the surface area of this cuboid.

$5n$ is the mean of the three numbers 391, $n$ and $n - 1$.
Find the value of $n$.

Factorise.

(a) $12x + 15$

(b) $xy - 2x + 3y - 6$

A is the point $(2, 1)$ and B is the point $(9, 4)$.
Find the length of AB.

A straight line joins the points $(3k, 6)$ and $(k, -5)$.
The line has a gradient of 2.
Find the value of $k$.

Find the nth term of each sequence.

(a) $\dfrac{1}{2}, \dfrac{1}{4}, \dfrac{1}{6}, \dfrac{1}{8}, \dfrac{1}{10}, \ldots$

(b) $1, 5, 25, 125, 625, \ldots$

Without using a calculator, work out $\dfrac{2}{3} + \dfrac{1}{4} \times \dfrac{2}{3}$.
Write down all the steps of your working and give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.

Ten athletes compete in both the 100 metre race and the triple jump.
Their results are shown in the scatter diagram.

% Placeholder for scatter diagram - needs to be extracted from PDF

(a) One of these athletes jumps 15.12 m in the triple jump.
Write down his time for the 100 metre race.

 s

(b) The values for two other athletes are shown in the table.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Distance in the triple jump (m) & Time for 100 m (seconds) \\
\hline
14.74 & 13.2 \\
15.2 & 12.76 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

On the scatter diagram, plot these points.

(c) On the scatter diagram, draw a line of best fit.

(d) What type of correlation is shown in the scatter diagram?

tex:
By shading the unwanted regions on the grid, draw and label the region R that satisfies the following inequalities.

$-2 \leq x \leq 3$

$y \leq x + 3$

The diagram shows the speed--time graph for 70 seconds of a car journey.

% Placeholder for speed-time graph - needs to be extracted from PDF

(a) Calculate the deceleration of the car during the first 20 seconds.

 m/s$^2$

(b) Calculate the total distance travelled by the car during the 70 seconds.

 m

$t$ is inversely proportional to the square of $(x + 1)$.
When $x = 2$, $t = 5$.

(a) Write $t$ in terms of $x$.

(b) When $t = 1.8$, find the positive value of $x$.

(a) Work out the inverse of the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} -3 & 10 \\ 1 & -5 \end{pmatrix}$.

(b) Work out the value of $x$ and the value of $y$ in this matrix calculation.

$\begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 5 & -4 \\ y & 2 \end{pmatrix} \begin{pmatrix} x \\ 1 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 9 \\ 46 \end{pmatrix}$

 \quad

A container is made from a cylinder and a cone, each of radius 5 cm.
The height of the cylinder is 12 cm and the height of the cone is 4.8 cm.

% Placeholder for diagram - needs to be extracted from PDF

The cylinder is filled completely with water.
The container is turned upside down as shown below.

Calculate the depth, $d$, of the water.

[The volume, $V$, of a cone with radius $r$ and height $h$ is $V = \dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$.]

 cm

The time, $t$ minutes, it takes each of 50 students to travel to school is recorded.
The table shows the results.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ minutes) & Frequency \\
\hline
$0 \leq t < 10$ & 7 \\
$10 \leq t < 15$ & 19 \\
$15 \leq t < 20$ & 16 \\
$20 \leq t < 40$ & 8 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) Write down the modal class.

(b) On the grid, complete the histogram to show the information in the table.
texHtml:
The time, $t$ minutes, it takes each of 50 students to travel to school is recorded. The table shows the results.
Time ($t$ minutes)Frequency
$0 \leq t < 10$7
$10 \leq t < 15$19
$15 \leq t < 20$16
$20 \leq t < 40$8
(a) Write down the modal class. (b) On the grid, complete the histogram to show the information in the table.
